title: Pristiglottis serriformis
intro: Pristiglottis serriformis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae, commonly known as a type of fern or a vining plant native to tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, with its extracts showing potential in alleviating inflammation, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing due to its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Pristiglottis serriformis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, with the most common herbal preparations being decoctions and infusions used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Pristiglottis serriformis is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the spleenwort family. It belongs to the genus Pristiglottis, which consists of several species of ferns. The classification of Pristiglottis serriformis includes its kingdom as Plantae, followed by the division Pteridophyta, and the class Polypodiopsida. This fern is native to tropical regions, particularly in Southeast Asia. Pristiglottis serriformis is characterized by its unique serrated fronds and is an epiphytic plant, growing on the trunks and branches of trees.

preparations: Pristiglottis serriformis preparations are often used in traditional medicine, typically made into a paste or infused in hot water.
